The classification header has already been emitted. Do NOT emit it again. This skill handles **execution only** for STANDARD and STRICT tier tasks.\n>\n> If for any reason no classification header was emitted before this skill was invoked, emit one now using ONLY the tier values STRICT, STANDARD, LIGHT, or EXEMPT (no other values are valid).\n\n# /sc:task-unified - Unified Task Execution with Compliance\n\n## Purpose\n\nUnified command that merges orchestration capabilities with MCP compliance enforcement. Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- Apply STRICT overhead to genuinely trivial changes\n- Override user's explicit compliance choice\n- Proceed with <70% confidence without user confirmation\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Tier classification accuracy | >=80% | User feedback on appropriateness |\n| User confusion rate | <10% | \"Which command?\" questions eliminated |\n| Skip rate (--skip-compliance) | <12% | Override tracking |\n| Regression prevention | >=85% | Post-verification bug detection |\n| STRICT tier overhead | <25% | Execution telemetry |\n\n## Configuration References\n\n- Keywords: `config/tier-keywords.yaml`\n- Verification routing: `config/verification-routing.yaml`\n- Acceptance criteria: `config/tier-acceptance-criteria.yaml`\n- Circuit breakers: See MCP.md\n- Routing logic: See ORCHESTRATOR.md\n\n\nARGUMENTS: Execute all tasks in 331→@dataclass\n   332→class TurnLedger:\n   333→    \"\"\"Economic model for subprocess turn budget tracking.\n   334→\n   335→    Tracks budget allocation, consumption, and reimbursement for Enforces monotonicity: consumed can only increase.\n   337→    \"\"\"\n   338→\n   339→    initial_budget: int\n   340→    consumed: int = 0\n   341→    reimbursed: int = 0\n   342→    reimbursement_rate: float = 0.5\n   343→    minimum_allocation: int = 5\n   344→    minimum_remediation_budget: int = 3\n   345→\n   346→    def available(self) -> int:\n   347→        \"\"\"Return available turns: initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n   348→        return self.initial_budget - self.consumed + self.reimbursed\n   349→\n   350→    def debit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n   351→        \"\"\"Consume turns from the budget. Enforces monotonicity.\"\"\"\n   352→        if turns < 0:\n   353→            raise ValueError(\"debit amount must be non-negative\")\n   354→        self.consumed += turns\n   355→\n   356→    def credit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n   357→        \"\"\"Reimburse turns to the budget.\"\"\"\n   358→        if turns < 0:\n   359→            raise ValueError(\"credit amount must be non-negative\")\n   360→        self.reimbursed += turns\n   361→\n   362→    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n   363→        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for a subprocess launch.\"\"\"\n   364→        return self.available() >= self.minimum_allocation\n   365→\n   366→    def can_remediate(self) -> bool:\n   367→        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for remediation.\"\"\"\n   368→        return self.available() >= self.minimum_remediation_budget\n   369→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019iExz5v9QH9BVzi4dhocBE","session_id":"d6f22df6-6272-49ce-85eb-24165e9ecc64","uuid":"22031970-c7e7-4fc9-851d-e2e8a53f978e"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_LxoIwpJVG3PJdJ3XFlCXSFyt","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Sidecar output monitor — daemon thread that watches output files.\n     2→\n     3→Parses stream-json (NDJSON) output from ``claude --print --output-format stream-json``.\n     4→Each line is a JSON object with a ``type`` field.  The monitor extracts task IDs,\n     5→tool names, and file-change signals from these events to drive the TUI.\n     6→\"\"\"\n     7→\n     8→from __future__ import annotations\n     9→\n    10→import json\n    11→import logging\n    12→import re\n    13→import threading\n    14→import time\n    15→from pathlib import Path\n    16→\n    17→from .debug_logger import debug_log\n    18→from .models import MonitorState\n    19→\n    20→_dbg = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.sprint.debug.monitor\")\n    21→\n    22→# Patterns to extract from stringified event content\n    23→TASK_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"T\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\")\n    24→TOOL_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    25→    r\"\\b(Read|Edit|MultiEdit|Write|Grep|Glob|Bash|TodoWrite|TodoRead|Task)\\b\"\n    26→)\n    27→FILES_CHANGED_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    28→    r\"(?:modified|created|edited|wrote|updated)\\s+[`'\\\"]?([^\\s`'\\\"]+\\.\\w+)\"\n    29→)\n    30→\n    31→# Pattern for detecting budget exhaustion in NDJSON output\n    32→ERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"subtype\"\\s*:\\s*\"error_max_turns\"')\n    33→\n    34→\n    35→def detect_error_max_turns(output_path: Path) -> bool:\n    36→    \"\"\"Check if the last NDJSON line indicates budget exhaustion.\n    37→\n    38→    Scans the last non-empty line of the output file for the\n    39→    ``\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"`` pattern, which signals that a\n    40→    subprocess exhausted its turn budget.\n    41→\n    42→    Returns True if error_max_turns is detected, False otherwise.\n    43→    \"\"\"\n    44→    try:\n    45→        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    46→    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n    47→        return False\n    48→\n    49→    if not content.strip():\n    50→        return False\n    51→\n    52→    # Get last non-empty line\n    53→    lines = content.strip().splitlines()\n    54→    for line in reversed(lines):\n    55→        line = line.strip()\n    56→        if line:\n    57→            return bool(ERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN.search(line))\n    58→\n    59→    return False\n    60→\n    61→\n    62→# Pattern for counting assistant message turns in NDJSON output\n    63→# Each \"assistant\" type message represents one turn consumed.\n    64→_TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"type\"\\s*:\\s*\"assistant\"')\n    65→\n    66→\n    67→def count_turns_from_output(output_path: Path) -> int:\n    68→    \"\"\"Extract the number of turns consumed from subprocess NDJSON output.\n    69→\n    70→    Counts lines containing ``\"type\":\"assistant\"`` which represent\n    71→    individual assistant response turns. Each such line indicates\n    72→    one turn was consumed from the budget.\n    73→\n    74→    Args:\n    75→        output_path: Path to the subprocess NDJSON output file.\n    76→\n    77→    Returns:\n    78→        Number of turns counted. Returns 0 if file is missing or empty.\n    79→    \"\"\"\n    80→    try:\n    81→        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    82→    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n    83→        return 0\n    84→\n    85→    if not content.strip():\n    86→        return 0\n    87→\n    88→    count = 0\n    89→    for line in content.splitlines():\n    90→        line = line.strip()\n    91→        if line and _TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN.search(line):\n    92→            count += 1\n    93→\n    94→    return count\n    95→\n    96→\n    97→class OutputMonitor:\n    98→    \"\"\"Background thread that watches a stream-json output file.\n    99→\n   100→    Reads incremental bytes, splits on newlines, and parses each complete\n   101→    line as JSON.  Partial lines are buffered across poll cycles so no\n   102→    data is lost when a write straddles a poll boundary.\n   103→    \"\"\"\n   104→\n   105→    def __init__(self, output_path: Path, 144→        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n   145→            self._poll_once()\n   146→            self._stop_event.wait(self.poll_interval)\n   147→\n   148→    def _poll_once(self):\n   149→ Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n    93→\n    94→    All directories are created if they don't exist. Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n   122→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   123→\n   124→@dataclass\n   125→class AggregatedPhaseReport:\n   126→    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n   127→\n   128→    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n   129→    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking even when subprocesses\n   130→    are budget-exhausted.\n   131→    \"\"\"\n   132→\n   133→    phase_number: int\n   134→    tasks_total: int = 0\n   135→  @property\n   145→    def status(self) -> str:\n   146→        \"\"\"Overall phase status: PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n   147→        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n   148→            return \"PASS\"\n   149→        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n   150→            return \"PASS\"\n   151→        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n   152→            return \"FAIL\"\n   153→ \"|---------|-------|--------|-------|----------|\",\n   170→        ]\n   171→        for tr in self.task_results:\n   172→            dur = f\"{tr.duration_seconds:.1f}s\"\n   173→ 194→\n   195→\n   196→def aggregate_task_results(\n   197→    phase_number: int,\n   198→    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n   199→    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n   200→) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n   201→    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n   202→\n   203→    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n   204→    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n   205→\n   206→    Args:\n   207→        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n   208→        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n   209→        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n   210→\n   211→    Returns:\n   212→        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n   213→    \"\"\"\n   214→    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n   215→        phase_number=phase_number,\n   216→        task_results=task_results,\n   217→       1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS\n   223→    )\n   224→    report.tasks_failed = sum(\n   225→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n   226→    )\n   227→    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n   228→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   229→    )\n   230→    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n   231→        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n   232→    )\n   233→    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n   234→    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(\n   235→        r.duration_seconds for r in task_results\n   236→    )\n   237→\n   238→    return report\n   239→\n   240→\n   241→def check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n   242→    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n   243→\n   244→    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n   245→    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n   246→    \"\"\"\n   247→    if ledger is None:\n   248→        return None\n   249→    if ledger.can_launch():\n   250→        return None\n   251→ 257→def execute_phase_tasks(\n   258→    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n   259→    config: SprintConfig,\n   260→    phase,\n   261→    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n   262→    *,\n   263→    _subprocess_factory=None,\n   264→) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n   265→    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n   266→\n   267→    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n   268→    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n   269→    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n   270→\n   271→    Args:\n   272→        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n   273→        config: Sprint configuration.\n   274→        phase: The Phase being executed.\n   275→        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n   276→        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n   277→            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n   278→\n   279→    Returns:\n   280→        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n   281→        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n   282→    \"\"\"\n   283→    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n   284→    remaining: list[str] = []\n   285→\n   286→    if not tasks:\n   287→        return results, remaining\n   288→\n   289→    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n   290→        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   291→\n   292→        # Budget check: can we launch?\n   293→        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n   294→            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n   295→            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n   296→            for t in tasks[i:]:\n   297→                results.append(\n   298→                    TaskResult(\n   299→                        task=t,\n   300→                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n   301→                        started_at=started_at,\n   302→                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   303→                    )\n   304→                )\n   305→            break\n   306→\n   307→        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n   308→        if ledger is not None:\n   309→            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n   310→\n   311→        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n   312→        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n   313→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n   314→                task, config, phase\n   315→            )\n   316→        else:\n   317→            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n   318→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n   319→                task, config, phase\n   320→            )\n   321→\n   322→        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   323→\n   324→        # Determine task status from exit code\n   325→        if exit_code == 0:\n   326→            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n   327→        elif exit_code == 124:\n   328→            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   329→        else:\n   330→            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n   331→\n   332→        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n   333→        if ledger is not None:\n   334→            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n   335→            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n   336→            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n   337→            if actual > pre_allocated:\n   338→                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n   339→            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n   340→                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n   341→\n   342→        results.append(\n   343→            TaskResult(\n   344→                task=task,\n   345→                status=status,\n   346→                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n   347→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   348→                started_at=started_at,\n   349→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   350→                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n   351→            )\n   352→        )\n   353→\n   354→    return results, remaining\n   355→\n   356→\n   357→def _run_task_subprocess(\n   358→    task: TaskEntry,\n   359→    config: SprintConfig,\n   360→    phase,\n   361→) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n   362→    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n   363→\n   364→    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n   365→    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n   366→    \"\"\"\n   367→    # Build a task-specific prompt\n   368→    prompt = (\n   369→        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n   370→        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n   371→        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n   372→    )\n   373→\n   374→    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n   375→    proc.config = config\n   376→    proc.phase = phase\n   377→    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n   378→    _Base.__init__(\n   379→        proc,\n   380→        prompt=prompt,\n   381→        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   382→        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   383→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   384→        model=config.model,\n   385→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   386→        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n   387→        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n   388→    )\n   389→    proc.start()\n   390→    proc.wait()\n   391→    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n   392→    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   393→    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n   394→    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n   395→    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n   396→\n   397→\n   398→def execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n   399→    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n   400→\n   401→    For each active phase:\n   402→    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n   403→    2. Start output monitor thread\n   404→    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n   405→    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n   406→    5. Record PhaseResult\n   407→    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n   408→    \"\"\"\n   409→    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n   410→    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n   411→        raise SystemExit(\n   412→            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n   413→            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n   414→        )\n   415→\n   416→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   417→    signal_handler.install()\n   418→\n   419→    setup_debug_logger(config)\n   420→    import logging as _logging\n   421→    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n   422→\n   423→    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n   424→    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n   425→    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n   426→    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n   427→\n   428→    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n   429→    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n   430→\n   431→    tui.start()\n   432→\n   433→    try:\n   434→        for phase in config.active_phases:\n   435→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   436→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   437→                break\n   438→\n   439→            # Reset monitor for this phase\n   440→            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   441→            monitor.reset(output_path)\n   442→            monitor.start()\n   443→\n   444→            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n   445→            if config.tmux_session_name:\n   446→                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n   447→\n   448→            # Launch claude\n   449→            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n   450→            proc_manager.start()\n   451→            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   452→            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n   453→            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n   454→            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n   455→\n   456→            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n   457→\n   458→            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   459→\n   460→            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n   461→            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n   462→            _timed_out = False\n   463→            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n   464→            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n   465→            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n   466→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   467→                    proc_manager.terminate()\n   468→                    break\n   469→                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n   470→                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n   471→                    _timed_out = True\n   472→                    proc_manager.terminate()\n   473→                    break\n   474→\n   475→                ms = monitor.state\n   476→                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n   477→\n   478→                debug_log(\n   479→                    _dbg,\n   480→                    \"poll_tick\",\n   481→                    phase=phase.number,\n   482→                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   483→                    poll_result=\"running\",\n   484→                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n   485→                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n   486→                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n   487→                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   488→                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n   489→                )\n   490→\n   491→                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n   492→                if (\n   493→                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n   494→                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n   495→                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n   496→                    and not _stall_acted\n   497→                ):\n   498→                    _stall_acted = True\n   499→                    debug_log(\n   500→                        _dbg,\n   501→                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n   502→                        phase=phase.number,\n   503→                        action=config.stall_action,\n   504→                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   505→                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   506→                    )\n   507→                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n   508→                        import sys\n   509→                        print(\n   510→                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   511→                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n   512→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   513→                        )\n   514→                        _timed_out = True\n   515→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   516→                        break\n   517→                    else:\n   518→                        # warn action: log and continue\n   519→                        import sys\n   520→                        print(\n   521→                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   522→                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n   523→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   524→                        )\n   525→\n   526→                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n   527→                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n   528→                    _stall_acted = False\n   529→\n   530→                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n   531→                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n   532→                try:\n   533→                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   534→                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n   535→                    import sys\n   536→                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n   537→                time.sleep(0.5)\n   538→\n   539→            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n   540→            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n   541→            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n   542→            if _timed_out:\n   543→                exit_code = 124\n   544→            else:\n   545→                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n   546→            monitor.stop()\n   547→            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   548→            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   549→            debug_log(\n   550→                _dbg,\n   551→                \"PHASE_END\",\n   552→                phase=phase.number,\n   553→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   554→                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   555→            )\n   556→\n   557→            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n   558→            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n   559→            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n   560→            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n   561→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   562→                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n   563→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   564→                break\n   565→\n   566→            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n   574→            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n   575→            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n   576→\n   577→            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n   578→                phase=phase,\n   579→                status=status,\n   580→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   581→                started_at=started_at,\n   582→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   583→                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n   584→                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n   585→                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n   586→                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n   587→            )\n   588→            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n   589→\n   590→            debug_log(\n   591→                _dbg,\n   592→                \"phase_complete\",\n   593→                phase=phase.number,\n   594→                status=status.value,\n   595→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   596→                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   597→            )\n   598→\n   599→            # Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n   682→    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n   683→    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n   684→    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n   685→    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n   686→    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n   687→    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n   688→    \"\"\"\n   689→    if exit_code == 124:\n   690→        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n   691→    if exit_code != 0:\n   692→        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n   693→\n   694→    if result_file.exists():\n   695→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n   696→        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n   697→        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n   698→        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n   699→        upper = content.upper()\n   700→        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n   701→        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n   702→        if has_halt:\n   703→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   704→        if has_continue:\n   705→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n   706→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   707→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n   708→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   709→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   710→        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n   711→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   712→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   713→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n   714→\n   715→    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n   716→        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n   717→        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n   718→        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n   719→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n   720→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   721→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n   722→\n   723→    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n   724→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019iExz5v9QH9BVzi4dhocBE","session_id":"d6f22df6-6272-49ce-85eb-24165e9ecc64","uuid":"5217bcb7-5247-4192-b36c-051741fd4d44"}

`/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint data models — enums, dataclasses, and pure-data types.\n\nAll other sprint modules depend on these types. now = time.monotonic()\n        if self.events_received == 0:\n            # No events yet — subprocess is starting up\n            if now - self.phase_started_at > 120:\n                return \"STALLED\"\n            return \"waiting...\"\n        # Events have been flowing — check time since last event\n        since_last = now - self.last_event_time\n        if since_last > 120:\n            return \"STALLED\"\n        if since_last > 30:\n            return \"thinking...\"\n        return \"active\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_size_display(self) -> str:\n        if f\"{self.output_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TurnLedger:\n    \"\"\"Economic model for subprocess turn budget tracking.\n\n    Tracks budget allocation, consumption, and reimbursement for sprint\n    subprocesses. Enforces monotonicity: consumed can only increase.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    initial_budget: int\n    consumed: int = 0\n    reimbursed: int = 0\n    reimbursement_rate: float = 0.5\n    minimum_allocation: int = 5\n    minimum_remediation_budget: int = 3\n\n    def available(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Return available turns: initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n        return self.initial_budget - self.consumed + self.reimbursed\n\n    def debit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Consume turns from the budget. Enforces monotonicity.\"\"\"\n        if turns < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"debit amount must be non-negative\")\n        self.consumed += turns\n\n    def credit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reimburse turns to the budget.\"\"\"\n        if turns < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"credit amount must be non-negative\")\n        self.reimbursed += turns\n\n    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for a subprocess launch.\"\"\"\n        return self.available() >= self.minimum_allocation\n\n    def can_remediate(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for remediation.\"\"\"\n        return self.available() >= self.minimum_remediation_budget\n```\n\nFile: 4-Layer Subprocess Isolation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Configuration for the 4-layer subprocess isolation.\n\n    Each layer prevents cross-task state leakage:\n    1. scoped_work_dir: Restrict working directory to release dir\n    2. git_boundary: Set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to prevent upward traversal\n    3. empty_plugin_dir: Point CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR to an empty tempdir\n    4. restricted_settings: Set CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR to an isolated tempdir\n\n    The ``env_vars`` property returns a dict of environment variable overrides\n    that should be merged into the subprocess environment.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    scoped_work_dir: Path\n    git_boundary: Path\n    plugin_dir: Path\n    settings_dir: Path\n\n    @property\n    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(self.scoped_work_dir),\n            \"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\": str(self.git_boundary),\n            \"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\": str(self.plugin_dir),\n            \"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\": str(self.settings_dir),\n        }\n\n    @property\n    def layers_active(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of active isolation layer names for verification.\"\"\"\n        active = []\n        if self.scoped_work_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"scoped_work_dir\")\n        if self.git_boundary.exists():\n            active.append(\"git_boundary\")\n        if self.plugin_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"empty_plugin_dir\")\n        if self.settings_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"restricted_settings\")\n        return active\n\n\ndef setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n\n    Sets up:\n    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n\n    All directories are created if they don't exist. The caller is\n    responsible for passing ``layers.env_vars`` to the subprocess.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing the release directory.\n\n    Returns:\n        IsolationLayers with all 4 layers configured.\n    \"\"\"\n    base = config.results_dir / \".isolation\"\n    base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    plugin_dir = base / \"plugins\"\n Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n\n    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking even when subprocesses\n    are budget-exhausted.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    phase_number: int\n    tasks_total: int = 0\n    tasks_passed: int = 0\n    tasks_failed: int = 0\n    tasks_incomplete: int = 0\n    tasks_skipped: int = 0\n    total_turns_consumed: int = 0\n    total_duration_seconds: float = 0.0\n    task_results: list[TaskResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def status(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Overall phase status: PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n            return \"FAIL\"\n        return \"PARTIAL\"\n\n    def to_markdown(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a markdown string with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            \"---\",\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n            \"---\",\n            \"\",\n            f\"# Phase {self.phase_number} — Aggregated Task Report\",\n            \"\",\n            \"| Task ID | Title | Status | Turns | Duration |\",\n            \"|---------|-------|--------|-------|----------|\",\n        ]\n        for tr in self.task_results:\n            dur = f\"{tr.duration_seconds:.1f}s\"\n            lines.append(\n                f\"| {tr.task.task_id} | {tr.task.title} | {tr.status.value} \"\n                f\"| {tr.turns_consumed} | {dur} |\"\n            )\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total turns consumed:** {self.total_turns_consumed}\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total duration:** {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}s\")\n\n        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Remaining Tasks (Budget Exhausted)\")\n            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n                lines.append(f\"- {tid}\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        if self.status == \"PASS\":\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        else:\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n\n\ndef aggregate_task_results(\n    phase_number: int,\n    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n\n    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n\n    Args:\n        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n\n    Returns:\n        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n        phase_number=phase_number,\n        task_results=task_results,\n        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n    )\n\n    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.tasks_passed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS\n    )\n    report.tasks_failed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n    )\n    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    )\n    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n    )\n    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(\n        r.duration_seconds for r in task_results\n    )\n\n    return report\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        results.append(\n            TaskResult(\n                task=task,\n                status=status,\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this phase\n            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n            if config.tmux_session_name:\n                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n            # Launch claude\n            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n            proc_manager.start()\n            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n            _timed_out = False\n            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n```\n\nFile: `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/process.py`\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint process management — subprocess lifecycle and signal handling.\n\nClaudeProcess extends pipeline.process.ClaudeProcess with sprint-specific\nconstructor (config, phase) and build_prompt(). \"\"\"Factory returning an on_signal closure for sprint debug logging.\n\n    Logs signal_sent events with the signal name and process id.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def on_signal(pid: int, signal_name: str) debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n            \"exit\",\n            pid=pid,\n            code=returncode,\n            was_timeout=(returncode == 124),\n        )\n\n    return on_exit\n\n\nclass ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess):\n    \"\"\"Sprint-specific claude process extending the pipeline base.\n\n    Defines only __init__ (with hook wiring) and build_prompt().\n    All subprocess lifecycle (start, wait, terminate) is inherited\n    from the pipeline base class; sprint debug logging is injected\n    via lifecycle hook factories.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase):\n        self.config = config\n        self.phase = phase\n        prompt = self.build_prompt()\n        super().__init__(\n            prompt=prompt,\n            output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n            max_turns=config.max_turns,\n            model=config.model,\n            permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n            timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n            output_format=\"stream-json\",\n            on_spawn=_make_spawn_hook(phase, config),\n            on_signal=_make_signal_hook(phase, config),\n            on_exit=_make_exit_hook(phase, config),\n        )\n\n    def build_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the /sc:task-unified prompt for this phase.\"\"\"\n        pn = self.phase.number\n        result_file = self.config.result_file(self.phase)\n        phase_file = self.phase.file\n\n        return (\n            f\"/sc:task-unified Execute all tasks in @{phase_file} \"\n            f\"--compliance strict --strategy systematic\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Execution Rules\\n\"\n            f\"- Execute tasks in order (T{pn:02d}XX.01, T{pn:02d}XX.02, etc.)\\n\"\n            f\"- For STRICT tier tasks: use Sequential MCP for analysis, \"\n            f\"run quality verification\\n\"\n            f\"- For STANDARD tier tasks: run direct test execution per \"\n            f\"acceptance criteria\\n\"\n            f\"- For LIGHT tier tasks: quick sanity check only\\n\"\n            f\"- For EXEMPT tier tasks: skip formal verification\\n\"\n            f\"- If a STRICT-tier task fails, STOP and report -- \"\n            f\"do not continue to next task\\n\"\n            f\"- For all other tier failures, log the failure and continue\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Completion Protocol\\n\"\n            f\"When ALL tasks in this phase are complete \"\n            f\"(or halted on STRICT failure):\\n\"\n            f\"1. Write a phase completion report to {result_file} containing:\\n\"\n            f\"   - YAML frontmatter with: phase, status (PASS|FAIL|PARTIAL), \"\n            f\"tasks_total, tasks_passed, tasks_failed\\n\"\n            f\"   - Per-task status table: Task ID, Title, Tier, Status \"\n            f\"(pass/fail/skip), Evidence\\n\"\n            f\"   - Files modified (list all paths)\\n\"\n            f\"   - Blockers for next phase (if any)\\n\"\n            f\"   - The literal string EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE \"\n            f\"or EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\n\"\n            f\"2. If any task produced file changes, list them under \"\n            f\"## Files Modified\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Important\\n\"\n            f\"- This is Phase {pn} of a multi-phase sprint\\n\"\n            f\"- Previous phases have already been executed in separate sessions\\n\"\n            f\"- Do not re-execute work from prior phases\\n\"\n            f\"- Focus only on the tasks defined in the phase file\"\n        )\n\n\nclass SignalHandler:\n    \"\"\"Register signal handlers for graceful sprint shutdown.\n\n    On SIGINT/SIGTERM:\n    1. Set the shutdown flag (checked by the executor loop)\n    2. The executor terminates the current claude process\n    3. Writes partial execution log\n    4. Exits with appropriate code\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.shutdown_requested = False\n        self._original_sigint = None\n        self._original_sigterm = None\n\n    def install(self):\n        \"\"\"Install signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        self._original_sigint = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGINT)\n        self._original_sigterm = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle)\n\n    def uninstall(self):\n        \"\"\"Restore original signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        if self._original_sigint is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._original_sigint)\n        if self._original_sigterm is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._original_sigterm)\n\n    def _handle(self, signum, frame):\n        self.shutdown_requested = True\n```\n\nFile: `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/monitor.py`\n```python\n\"\"\"Sidecar output monitor — daemon thread that watches output files.\n\nParses stream-json (NDJSON) output from ``claude --print --output-format stream-json``.\nEach line is a JSON object with a ``type`` field.  The monitor extracts task IDs,\ntool names, and file-change signals from these events to drive the TUI.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .debug_logger import debug_log\nfrom .models import MonitorState\n\n_dbg = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.sprint.debug.monitor\")\n\n# Patterns to extract from stringified event content\nTASK_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"T\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\")\nTOOL_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(Read|Edit|MultiEdit|Write|Grep|Glob|Bash|TodoWrite|TodoRead|Task)\\b\"\n)\nFILES_CHANGED_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"(?:modified|created|edited|wrote|updated)\\s+[`'\\\"]?([^\\s`'\\\"]+\\.\\w+)\"\n)\n\n# Pattern for detecting budget exhaustion in NDJSON output\nERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"subtype\"\\s*:\\s*\"error_max_turns\"')\n\n\ndef detect_error_max_turns(output_path: Path) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if the last NDJSON line indicates budget exhaustion.\n\n    Scans the last non-empty line of the output file for the\n    ``\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"`` pattern, which signals that a\n    subprocess exhausted its turn budget.\n\n    Returns True if error_max_turns is detected, False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n        return False\n\n    if not content.strip():\n        return False\n\n    # Get last non-empty line\n    lines = content.strip().splitlines()\n    for line in reversed(lines):\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line:\n            return bool(ERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN.search(line))\n\n    return False\n\n\n# Pattern for counting assistant message turns in NDJSON output\n# Each \"assistant\" type message represents one turn consumed.\n_TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"type\"\\s*:\\s*\"assistant\"')\n\n\ndef count_turns_from_output(output_path: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Extract the number of turns consumed from subprocess NDJSON output.\n\n    Counts lines containing ``\"type\":\"assistant\"`` which represent\n    individual assistant response turns. Each such line indicates\n    one turn was consumed from the budget.\n\n    Args:\n        output_path: Path to the subprocess NDJSON output file.\n\n    Returns:\n        Number of turns counted. Returns 0 if file is missing or empty.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n        return 0\n\n    if not content.strip():\n        return 0\n\n    count = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line and _TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN.search(line):\n            count += 1\n\n    return count\n\n\nclass OutputMonitor:\n    \"\"\"Background thread that watches a stream-json output file.\n\n    Reads incremental bytes, splits on newlines, and parses each complete\n    line as JSON.  Partial lines are buffered across poll cycles so no\n    data is lost when a write straddles a poll boundary.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, output_path: Path, poll_interval: float = 0.5):\n        self.output_path = output_path\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._thread: threading.Thread | None = None\n        self._last_read_pos: int = 0\n        self._line_buffer: str = \"\"\n        self._seen_files: set[str] = set()\n\n    def start(self):\n        \"\"\"Start the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(\n            target=self._poll_loop,\n            daemon=True,\n            name=\"output-monitor\",\n        )\n        self._thread.start()\n\n    def stop(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join(timeout=2.0)\n\n    def reset(self, new_output_path: Path):\n        \"\"\"Reset for a new phase (new output file).\"\"\"\n        self.output_path = new_output_path\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n\n    def _poll_loop(self):\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            self._poll_once()\n            self._stop_event.wait(self.poll_interval)\n\n    def _poll_once(self):\n        now = time.monotonic()\n\n        try:\n            size = self.output_path.stat().st_size\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return\n\n        self.state.output_bytes_prev = self.state.output_bytes\n        self.state.output_bytes = size\n\n        if size > self._last_read_pos:\n            # New data available — read incremental chunk\n            chunk = self._read_new_chunk(size)\n            if chunk:\n                self._process_chunk(chunk, now)\n        else:\n            # No growth — update stall counter\n            self.state.stall_seconds = now - self.state.last_event_time\n\n        debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n            \"output_file_stat\",\n            path=str(self.output_path),\n            size=size,\n            events_received=self.state.events_received,\n            last_event_time=round(self.state.last_event_time, 1),\n        )\n\n        # Growth rate: exponential moving average\n        delta = self.state.output_bytes - self.state.output_bytes_prev\n        alpha = 0.3\n        self.state.growth_rate_bps = (\n            alpha * (delta / self.poll_interval)\n            + (1 - alpha) * self.state.growth_rate_bps\n        )\n\n    def _read_new_chunk(self, current_size: int) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read only the bytes added since last poll.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(self.output_path, errors=\"replace\") as f:\n                f.seek(self._last_read_pos)\n                chunk = f.read(current_size - self._last_read_pos)\n                self._last_read_pos = current_size\n                return chunk\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _process_chunk(self, chunk: str, now: float):\n        \"\"\"Split chunk into lines, parse complete NDJSON lines, buffer partials.\"\"\"\n        # Prepend any leftover partial line from previous poll\n        data = self._line_buffer + chunk\n        lines = data.split(\"\\n\")\n\n        # Last element is either \"\" (if chunk ended with \\n) or a partial line\n        self._line_buffer = lines[-1]\n\n        # Process all complete lines (everything except the last split element)\n        for line in lines[:-1]:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n\n            # Update liveness on every complete line\n            self.state.last_growth_time = now\n            self.state.last_event_time = now\n            self.state.stall_seconds = 0.0\n            self.state.events_received += 1\n            self.state.lines_total += 1\n\n            # Try to parse as JSON\n            try:\n                event = json.loads(line)\n            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n                # Not valid JSON — still counts as a line for liveness,\n                # but try text-mode signal extraction as fallback\n                self._extract_signals_from_text(line)\n                continue\n\n            self._extract_signals_from_event(event)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_event(self, event: dict):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from a parsed NDJSON event.\"\"\"\n        event_type = event.get(\"type\", \"\")\n        debug_log(_dbg, \"ndjson_line_parsed\", event_type=event_type, parsed=True)\n\n        # Tool use events: extract tool name directly\n        if event_type == \"tool_use\":\n            tool = event.get(\"tool\", \"\")\n            if tool:\n                self.state.last_tool_used = tool\n\n        # Stringify the event for regex-based signal extraction\n        # This catches task IDs and file changes regardless of event structure\n        text = json.dumps(event, default=str)\n        self._extract_signals_from_text(text)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_text(self, text: str):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from text using regex.\"\"\"\n        # Last task ID (take the last match)\n        task_matches = TASK_ID_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if task_matches:\n            self.state.last_task_id = task_matches[-1]\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_extracted\", signal_type=\"task_id\", value=task_matches[-1])\n\n        # Last tool used (only if not already set by structured extraction)\n        tool_matches = TOOL_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if tool_matches:\n            self.state.last_tool_used = tool_matches[-1]\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_extracted\", signal_type=\"tool_name\", value=tool_matches[-1])\n\n        # Files changed (accumulate unique paths)\n        file_matches = FILES_CHANGED_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        for f in file_matches:\n            self._seen_files.add(f)\n        self.state.files_changed = len(self._seen_files)\n```\n\nFile: `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_models.py`\n```python\n\"\"\"Unit
