False\n\n    def test_debit_zero_is_noop(self):\n        \"\"\"debit(0) is valid and does not change state.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.debit(0)\n        assert ledger.consumed == 0\n        assert ledger.available() == 50\n\n    def test_credit_zero_is_noop(self):\n        \"\"\"credit(0) is valid and does not change state.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.credit(0)\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == 0\n        assert ledger.available() == 50\n\n    def test_credit_exceeds_consumed_increases_available(self):\n        \"\"\"Crediting more than consumed is allowed (increases available beyond initial).\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        ledger.credit(20)\n        assert ledger.available() == 60  # 50 - 10 + 20\n\n    def test_budget_monotonicity_consumed_never_decreases(self):\n        \"\"\"consumed only increases — there is no method to decrease it.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50)\n        ledger.debit(10)\n        ledger.debit(5)\n        ledger.credit(20)  # credit doesn't decrease consumed\n        assert ledger.consumed == 15  # still 10 + 5\n\n    def test_budget_monotonicity_across_many_operations(self):\n        \"\"\"consumed never decreases across 15+ mixed operations.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200)\n        prev_consumed = 0\n        ops = [\n            (\"debit\", 10), (\"debit\", 5), (\"credit\", 3),\n            (\"debit\", 20), (\"credit\", 10), (\"debit\", 1),\n            (\"credit\", 0), (\"debit\", 15), (\"debit\", 0),\n            (\"credit\", 5), (\"debit\", 8), (\"credit\", 2),\n            (\"debit\", 12), (\"debit\", 3), (\"credit\", 7),\n        ]\n        for op, amount in ops:\n            if op == \"debit\":\n                ledger.debit(amount)\n            else:\n                ledger.credit(amount)\n            assert ledger.consumed >= prev_consumed, (\n                f\"consumed decreased from {prev_consumed} to {ledger.consumed} \"\n                f\"after {op}({amount})\"\n            )\n            prev_consumed = ledger.consumed\n\n        # Verify final arithmetic\n        total_debits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"debit\")\n        total_credits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"credit\")\n        assert ledger.consumed == total_debits\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == total_credits\n        assert ledger.available() == 200 - total_debits + total_credits\n\n    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_launch(self):\n        \"\"\"Budget exactly at minimum_allocation allows launch.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=5, minimum_allocation=5)\n        assert ledger.can_launch() is True\n\n    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_remediate(self):\n        \"\"\"Budget exactly at minimum_remediation_budget allows remediation.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=3, minimum_remediation_budget=3)\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is True\n```\n\nFile: `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/tests/sprint/test_process.py`\n```python\n\"\"\"Tests for sprint   f\"- For STANDARD tier tasks: run direct test execution per \"\n            f\"acceptance criteria\\n\"\n            f\"- For LIGHT tier tasks: quick sanity check only\\n\"\n            f\"- For 194→\n   195→\n   196→def aggregate_task_results(\n   197→    phase_number: int,\n   198→    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n   199→    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n   200→) -> 257→def execute_phase_tasks(\n   258→    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n   259→    config: SprintConfig,\n   260→    phase,\n   261→    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n   262→    *,\n   263→    _subprocess_factory=None,\n   264→) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n   265→    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n   266→\n   267→    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n   268→    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n   269→    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n   270→\n   271→    Args:\n   272→        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n   273→        config: Sprint configuration.\n   274→        phase: The Phase being executed.\n   275→        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n   276→        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n   277→            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n   278→\n   279→    Returns:\n   280→        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n   281→        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n   282→    \"\"\"\n   283→    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n   284→    remaining: list[str] = []\n   285→\n   286→    if not tasks:\n   287→        return results, remaining\n   288→\n   289→    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n   290→        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   291→\n   292→        # Budget check: can we launch?\n   293→        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n   294→            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n   295→            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n   296→            for t in tasks[i:]:\n   297→                results.append(\n   298→                    TaskResult(\n   299→                        task=t,\n   300→                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n   301→                        started_at=started_at,\n   302→                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   303→                    )\n   304→                )\n   305→            break\n   306→\n   307→        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n   308→        if ledger is not None:\n   309→            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n   310→\n   311→        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n   312→        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n   313→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n   314→                task, config, phase\n   315→            )\n   316→        else:\n   317→            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n   318→            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n   319→                task, config, phase\n   320→            )\n   321→\n   322→        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   323→\n   324→        # Determine task status from exit code\n   325→        if exit_code == 0:\n   326→            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n   327→        elif exit_code == 124:\n   328→            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   329→        else:\n   330→            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n   331→\n   332→        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n   333→        if ledger is not None:\n   334→            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n   335→            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n   336→            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n   337→            if actual > pre_allocated:\n   338→                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n   339→            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n   340→                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n   341→\n   342→        results.append(\n   343→            TaskResult(\n   344→                task=task,\n   345→                status=status,\n   346→                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n   347→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   348→                started_at=started_at,\n   349→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   350→                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n   351→            )\n   352→        )\n   353→\n   354→    return results, remaining\n   355→\n   356→\n   357→def _run_task_subprocess(\n   358→    task: TaskEntry,\n   359→    config: SprintConfig,\n   360→    phase,\n   361→) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n   362→    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n   363→\n   364→    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n   365→    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n   366→    \"\"\"\n   367→    # Build a task-specific prompt\n   368→    prompt = (\n   369→        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n   370→        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n   371→        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n   372→    )\n   373→\n   374→    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n   375→    proc.config = config\n   376→    proc.phase = phase\n   377→    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n   378→    _Base.__init__(\n   379→        proc,\n   380→        prompt=prompt,\n   381→        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n   382→        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n   383→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   384→        model=config.model,\n   385→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   386→        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n   387→        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n   388→    )\n   389→    proc.start()\n   390→    proc.wait()\n   391→    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n   392→    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   393→    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n   394→    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n   395→    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n   396→\n   397→\n   398→def execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n   399→    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n   400→\n   401→    For each active phase:\n   402→    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n   403→    2. Start output monitor thread\n   404→    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n   405→    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n   406→    5. Record PhaseResult\n   407→    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n   408→    \"\"\"\n   409→    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n   410→    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n   411→        raise SystemExit(\n   412→            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n   413→            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n   414→        )\n   415→\n   416→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   417→    signal_handler.install()\n   418→\n   419→    setup_debug_logger(config)\n   420→    import logging as _logging\n   421→    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n   422→\n   423→    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n   424→    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n   425→    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n   426→    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n   427→\n   428→    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n   429→    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n   430→\n   431→    tui.start()\n   432→\n   433→    try:\n   434→        for phase in config.active_phases:\n   435→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   436→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   437→                break\n   438→\n   439→            # Reset monitor for this phase\n   440→            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n   441→            monitor.reset(output_path)\n   442→            monitor.start()\n   443→\n   444→            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n   445→            if config.tmux_session_name:\n   446→                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n   447→\n   448→            # Launch claude\n   449→            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n   450→            proc_manager.start()\n   451→            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   452→            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n   453→            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n   454→            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n   455→\n   456→            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n   457→\n   458→            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   459→\n   460→            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n   461→            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n   462→            _timed_out = False\n   463→            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n   464→            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n   465→            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n   466→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   467→                    proc_manager.terminate()\n   468→                    break\n   469→                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n   470→                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n   471→                    _timed_out = True\n   472→                    proc_manager.terminate()\n   473→                    break\n   474→\n   475→                ms = monitor.state\n   476→                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n   477→\n   478→                debug_log(\n   479→                    _dbg,\n   480→                    \"poll_tick\",\n   481→                    phase=phase.number,\n   482→                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   483→                    poll_result=\"running\",\n   484→                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n   485→                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n   486→                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n   487→                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   488→                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n   489→                )\n   490→\n   491→                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n   492→                if (\n   493→                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n   494→                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n   495→                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n   496→                    and not _stall_acted\n   497→                ):\n   498→                    _stall_acted = True\n   499→                    debug_log(\n   500→                        _dbg,\n   501→                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n   502→                        phase=phase.number,\n   503→                        action=config.stall_action,\n   504→                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n   505→                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n   506→                    )\n   507→                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n   508→                        import sys\n   509→                        print(\n   510→                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   511→                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n   512→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   513→                        )\n   514→                        _timed_out = True\n   515→                        proc_manager.terminate()\n   516→                        break\n   517→                    else:\n   518→                        # warn action: log and continue\n   519→                        import sys\n   520→                        print(\n   521→                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n   522→                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n   523→                            file=sys.stderr,\n   524→                        )\n   525→\n   526→                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n   527→                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n   528→                    _stall_acted = False\n   529→\n   530→                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n   531→                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n   532→                try:\n   533→                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n   534→                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n   535→                    import sys\n   536→                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n   537→                time.sleep(0.5)\n   538→\n   539→            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n   540→            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n   541→            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n   542→            if _timed_out:\n   543→                exit_code = 124\n   544→            else:\n   545→                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n   546→            monitor.stop()\n   547→            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   548→            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   549→            debug_log(\n   550→                _dbg,\n   551→                \"PHASE_END\",\n   552→                phase=phase.number,\n   553→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   554→                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   555→            )\n   556→\n   557→            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n   558→            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n   559→            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n   560→            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n   561→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   562→                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n   563→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n   564→                break\n   565→\n   566→            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n   574→            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n   575→            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n   576→\n   577→            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n   578→                phase=phase,\n   579→                status=status,\n   580→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   581→                started_at=started_at,\n   582→                finished_at=finished_at,\n   583→                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n   584→                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n   585→                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n   586→                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n   587→            )\n   588→            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n   589→\n   590→            debug_log(\n   591→                _dbg,\n   592→                \"phase_complete\",\n   593→                phase=phase.number,\n   594→                status=status.value,\n   595→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   596→                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n   597→            )\n   598→\n   599→            # Decide: continue or halt?\n   606→            if status.is_failure:\n   607→                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n   608→                try:\n   609→                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n   610→                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n   611→                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n   612→                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n   613→                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n   614→                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n   615→                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n   616→                    debug_log(\n   617→                        _dbg,\n   618→                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n   619→                        phase=phase.number,\n   620→                        category=bundle.category.value,\n   621→                        path=str(diag_path),\n   622→                    )\n   623→                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n   624→                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n   625→\n   626→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n   627→                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n   628→                break\n   629→\n   630→        # Sprint finished\n   631→        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   632→        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n   633→            # Verify all phases actually passed\n   634→            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n   635→                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n   636→\n   637→        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n   638→        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n   639→        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n   640→\n   641→    finally:\n   642→        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n   643→        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n   644→        try:\n   645→            monitor.stop()\n   646→        except Exception:\n   647→            pass\n   648→        if proc_manager is not None:\n   649→            try:\n   650→                proc_manager.terminate()\n   651→            except Exception:\n   652→                pass\n   653→        try:\n   654→            tui.stop()\n   655→        except Exception:\n   656→            pass\n   657→        try:\n   658→            signal_handler.uninstall()\n   659→        except Exception:\n   660→            pass\n   661→\n   662→    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n   663→    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n   664→    try:\n   665→        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n   666→    except OSError:\n   667→        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n   668→\n   669→    if _exitcode != 0:\n   670→        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n   671→\n   672→\n   673→def _determine_phase_status(\n   674→    exit_code: int,\n   675→    result_file: Path,\n   676→    output_file: Path,\n   677→) -> PhaseStatus:\n   678→    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n   679→\n   680→    Priority:\n   681→    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n   682→    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n   683→    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n   684→    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n   685→    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n   686→    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n   687→    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n   688→    \"\"\"\n   689→    if exit_code == 124:\n   690→        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n   691→    if exit_code != 0:\n   692→        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n   693→\n   694→    if result_file.exists():\n   695→        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n   696→        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n   697→        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n   698→        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n   699→        upper = content.upper()\n   700→        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n   701→        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n   702→        if has_halt:\n   703→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   704→        if has_continue:\n   705→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n   706→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   707→            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n   708→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   709→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   710→        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n   711→        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   712→            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n   713→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n   714→\n   715→    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n   716→        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n   717→        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n   718→        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n   719→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n   720→            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n   721→        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n   722→\n   723→    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n   724→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d6f22df6-6272-49ce-85eb-24165e9ecc64","uuid":"fccc1000-6c12-4b11-b8e3-04c80d2c134a","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/executor.py","content":"\"\"\"Sprint @property\n    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n\n    Sets up:\n    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n\n    All directories are created if they don't exist. The caller is\n    responsible for passing ``layers.env_vars`` to the subprocess.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing the release directory.\n\n    Returns:\n Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n\n    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking even when subprocesses\n    are budget-exhausted.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    phase_number: int\n    tasks_total: int = 0\n    tasks_passed: int = 0\n    tasks_failed: int = 0\n    tasks_incomplete: int = 0\n    tasks_skipped: int = 0\n    total_turns_consumed: int = 0\n    total_duration_seconds: float = 0.0\n    task_results: list[TaskResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def status(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Overall phase status: PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n            return \"FAIL\"\n        return \"PARTIAL\"\n\n    def to_markdown(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a markdown string with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            \"---\",\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n f\"| {tr.task.task_id} | {tr.task.title} | {tr.status.value} \"\n                f\"| {tr.turns_consumed} | {dur} |\"\n            )\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total turns consumed:** {self.total_turns_consumed}\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total duration:** {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}s\")\n\n        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Remaining Tasks (Budget Exhausted)\")\n            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n                lines.append(f\"- {tid}\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        if self.status == \"PASS\":\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        else:\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n\n\ndef aggregate_task_results(\n    phase_number: int,\n    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n\n    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n\n    Args:\n        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n\n    Returns:\n        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n        phase_number=phase_number,\n        task_results=task_results,\n        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n    )\n\n    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.tasks_passed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS\n    )\n    report.tasks_failed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n    )\n    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    )\n    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n    )\n    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(\n        r.duration_seconds for r in task_results\n    )\n\n    return report\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        results.append(\n            TaskResult(\n                task=task,\n                status=status,\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this phase\n            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n            if config.tmux_session_name:\n                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n            # Launch claude\n            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n            proc_manager.start()\n            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n            _timed_out = False\n            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","numLines":724,"startLine":1,"totalLines":724}}}

ledger.credit(20)  # credit doesn't decrease consumed\n   576→        assert ledger.consumed == 15  # still 10 + 5\n   577→\n   578→    def test_budget_monotonicity_across_many_operations(self):\n   579→        \"\"\"consumed never decreases across 15+ mixed operations.\"\"\"\n   580→        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200)\n   581→        prev_consumed = 0\n   582→        ops = [\n   583→            (\"debit\", 10), (\"debit\", 5), (\"credit\", 3),\n   584→            (\"debit\", 20), (\"credit\", 10), (\"debit\", 1),\n   585→            (\"credit\", 0), (\"debit\", 15), (\"debit\", 0),\n   586→            (\"credit\", 5), (\"debit\", 8), (\"credit\", 2),\n   587→            (\"debit\", 12), (\"debit\", 3), (\"credit\", 7),\n   588→        ]\n   589→        for op, amount in ops:\n   590→            if op == \"debit\":\n   591→                ledger.debit(amount)\n   592→            else:\n   593→                ledger.credit(amount)\n   594→            assert ledger.consumed >= prev_consumed, (\n   595→                f\"consumed decreased from {prev_consumed} to {ledger.consumed} \"\n   596→                f\"after {op}({amount})\"\n   597→            )\n   598→            prev_consumed = ledger.consumed\n   599→\n   600→        # Verify final arithmetic\n   601→        total_debits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"debit\")\n   602→        total_credits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"credit\")\n   603→        assert ledger.consumed == total_debits\n   604→        assert ledger.reimbursed == total_credits\n   605→        assert ledger.available() == 200 - total_debits + total_credits\n   606→\n   607→    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_launch(self):\n   608→        \"\"\"Budget exactly at minimum_allocation allows ledger.debit(10)\n        ledger.debit(5)\n        ledger.credit(20)  # credit doesn't decrease consumed\n        assert ledger.consumed == 15  # still 10 + 5\n\n    def test_budget_monotonicity_across_many_operations(self):\n        \"\"\"consumed never decreases across 15+ mixed operations.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=200)\n        prev_consumed = 0\n        ops = [\n            (\"debit\", 10), (\"debit\", 5), (\"credit\", 3),\n            (\"debit\", 20), (\"credit\", 10), (\"debit\", 1),\n            (\"credit\", 0), (\"debit\", 15), (\"debit\", 0),\n            (\"credit\", 5), (\"debit\", 8), (\"credit\", 2),\n            (\"debit\", 12), (\"debit\", 3), (\"credit\", 7),\n        ]\n        for op, amount in ops:\n            if op == \"debit\":\n                ledger.debit(amount)\n            else:\n                ledger.credit(amount)\n            assert ledger.consumed >= prev_consumed, (\n                f\"consumed decreased from {prev_consumed} to {ledger.consumed} \"\n                f\"after {op}({amount})\"\n            )\n            prev_consumed = ledger.consumed\n\n        # Verify final arithmetic\n        total_debits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"debit\")\n        total_credits = sum(a for o, a in ops if o == \"credit\")\n        assert ledger.consumed == total_debits\n        assert ledger.reimbursed == total_credits\n        assert ledger.available() == 200 - total_debits + total_credits\n\n    def test_exact_threshold_budget_can_launch(self):\n        \"\"\"Budget           \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\n\"\n        )\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n    def test_partial_status_produces_halt(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PARTIAL status in result file must produce HALT (existing behavior, regression guard).\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: PARTIAL\\n---\\n\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n    # --- error_max_turns reclassification tests ---\n\n    def test_reclassification_pass_no_report_with_error_max_turns(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PASS_NO_REPORT + error_max_turns → INCOMPLETE.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\n            '{\"type\":\"content\",\"text\":\"working...\"}\\n'\n            '{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"}\\n'\n        )\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n\n    def test_reclassification_pass_no_report_without_error_max_turns(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PASS_NO_REPORT without error_max_turns stays PASS_NO_REPORT.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\n            '{\"type\":\"content\",\"text\":\"working...\"}\\n'\n            '{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\"}\\n'\n        )\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    def test_reclassification_does_not_affect_normal_pass(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Result file with CONTINUE signal → PASS regardless of output content.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / output_file.write_text('{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"}\\n')\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n \"\"\"Tests for per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 3) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T02.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Task {i}\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T02.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always succeeds with 3 turns consumed.\"\"\"\n        return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _fail_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always fails.\"\"\"\n        return (1, 5, 512)\n\n    def test_per_task_spawns_one_subprocess_per_task(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        spawn_count = [0]\n\n        def counting_factory(task, config, phase):\n            spawn_count[0] += 1\n            return (0, 2, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=counting_factory\n        )\n        assert spawn_count[0] == 3\n        assert len(results) == 3\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_all_pass(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(2)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=self._pass_factory\n        )\n        assert all(r.status == TaskStatus.PASS for r in results)\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_budget_prevents_starvation(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"If budget insufficient for next task, HALT with remaining task IDs.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        # Budget allows only 1 task launch (min_allocation=5, budget=8)\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=8, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def consume_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 5, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=consume_factory,\n        )\n        # First task succeeds, next two should be skipped due to budget\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 1\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert remaining == [\"T02.02\", \"T02.03\"]\n\n    def test_per_task_budget_debit_credit(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"TurnLedger debit/credit occurs per-task.\"\"\"\n        config = ledger.available()\n\n        results, _ = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=self._pass_factory,\n        )\n        # Factory returns 3 turns consumed; ledger should reflect that\n        assert ledger.available() == initial - 3\n\n    def test_per_task_empty_inventory(self, tmp_path):\n        config = turns_consumed=turns,\n            exit_code=0 if status == TaskStatus.PASS else 1,\n        )\n\n    def test_aggregate_all_pass(self):\n        results = [\n            self._make_task_result(\"T02.01\", TaskStatus.PASS, 3),\n            self._make_task_result(\"T02.02\", TaskStatus.PASS, 5),\n        ]\n        report = aggregate_task_results(2, results)\n        assert report.tasks_total == 2\n        assert report.tasks_passed == 2\n        assert report.tasks_failed == 0\n        assert report.status == \"PASS\"\n        assert report.total_turns_consumed == 8\n\n    def test_aggregate_mixed_results(self):\n        results = [\n            self._make_task_result(\"T02.01\", TaskStatus.PASS, 3),\n            self._make_task_result(\"T02.02\", output\n        assert report.task_results is results\n        assert report.phase_number == 2\n\n\nclass TestTurnCountDebit:\n    \"\"\"Tests for turn counting and TurnLedger debit wiring integration.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_debit_called_with_correct_turns(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"ledger.debit() called with correct turn count after each task.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = [\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T02.01\", title=\"Task 1\"),\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T02.02\", title=\"Task 2\"),\n        ]\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 7, 100)  # 7 turns consumed per task\n\n        results, _ = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=factory,\n        )\n        # 2 tasks * 7 turns = 14 consumed\n        assert ledger.consumed == 14\n        assert ledger.available() == 100 - 14\n\n    def test_can_remediate_prevents_remediation_when_low(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"ledger.can_remediate() prevents remediation spawn when budget insufficient.\"\"\"\n        ledger = TurnLedger(\n            initial_budget=10,\n            minimum_allocation=5,\n            minimum_remediation_budget=3,\n        )\n        # Consume most of the budget\n        ledger.debit(8)\n        assert ledger.available() == 2\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is False\n\n    def test_can_remediate_allows_when_sufficient(self):\n        ledger = TurnLedger(\n            initial_budget=50,\n            minimum_allocation=5,\n            minimum_remediation_budget=3,\n        )\n        assert ledger.can_remediate() is True\n\n    def test_turn_count_zero_reimburses_minimum(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"If a task uses 0 turns, minimum_allocation is credited back.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = [TaskEntry(task_id=\"T02.01\", title=\"Task 1\")]\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def zero_turn_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 0, 100)  # 0 turns consumed\n\n        execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=zero_turn_factory,\n        )\n        # Pre-debited 5, actual 0, so credited 5 back\n        # Net effect: 0 consumed\n        assert ledger.available() == 50\n\n\nclass TestIntegrationSubprocess:\n    \"\"\"Integration test: full per-task subprocess loop with budget tracking.\n\n    Exercises ≥5 tasks through parse → allocate → launch → collect → aggregate\n    with budget verification. (D-0012)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 5) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T03.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Integration Task {i}\",\n                description=f\"Test task {i} for integration\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T03.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    def test_integration_subprocess_five_tasks_mixed_outcomes(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Full loop: 5 tasks with varied outcomes and budget accounting.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(5)\n\n        # Track per-task consumption: task 1=3, task 2=7, task 3=2, task 4=5, task 5=4\n        outcomes = [\n            (0, 3, 1024),    # PASS, 3 turns\n            (0, 7, 2048),    # PASS, 7 turns\n            (1, 2, 512),     # FAIL, 2 turns\n            (0, 5, 1536),    # PASS, 5 turns\n            (124, 4, 256),   # INCOMPLETE (timeout), 4 turns\n        ]\n        call_index = [0]\n\n        def mixed_factory(task, config, phase):\n            idx = call_index[0]\n            call_index[0] += 1\n            return outcomes[idx]\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_allocation=5)\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=mixed_factory,\n        )\n\n        # All 5 tasks launched (no budget exhaustion)\n        assert len(results) == 5\n        assert remaining == []\n        assert call_index[0] == 5\n\n        # Verify per-task statuses\n        assert results[0].status == TaskStatus.PASS\n        assert results[1].status == TaskStatus.PASS\n        assert results[2].status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n        assert results[3].status == TaskStatus.PASS\n        assert results[4].status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n\n        # Budget accounting: available reflects actual turn consumption\n        # The executor pre-debits minimum_allocation (5) per task, then reconciles.\n        # Net effect on available: initial - sum(actual_turns)\n        actual_turns = [3, 7, 2, 5, 4]\n        net_consumed = sum(actual_turns)  # 21\n        assert ledger.available() == 100 - net_consumed  # = 79\n        # Verify internal accounting is consistent\n        assert ledger.available() == ledger.initial_budget - ledger.consumed + ledger.reimbursed\n\n        # Aggregate into phase report\n        report = aggregate_task_results(\n            phase_number=1, task_results=results, remaining_task_ids=remaining\n        )\n        assert report.tasks_total == 5\n        assert report.tasks_passed == 3\n        assert report.tasks_failed == 1\n        assert report.tasks_incomplete == 1\n        assert report.status == \"PARTIAL\"\n        assert report.total_turns_consumed == sum(r.turns_consumed for r in results)\n\n    def test_integration_subprocess_budget_exhaustion_skips_remaining(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Budget exhaustion after 3 tasks skips remaining 2.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(5)\n\n        # Each task consumes 8 turns; budget=25, minimum_allocation=5\n        # Task 1: debit 5 upfront, then adjust to 8 → consumed=8, avail=17\n        # Task 2: debit 5 upfront, then adjust to 8 → consumed=16, avail=9\n        # Task 3: debit 5 upfront, then adjust to 8 → consumed=24, avail=1\n        # Task 4: can_launch? avail=1 < min_alloc=5 → SKIP\n        def heavy_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 8, 500)\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=25, minimum_allocation=5)\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=heavy_factory,\n        )\n\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 3\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert remaining == [\"T03.04\", \"T03.05\"]\n\n        # Aggregate reflects skipped tasks\n        report = aggregate_task_results(1, results, remaining)\n        assert report.tasks_total == 7  # 5 results + 2 remaining\n        assert report.tasks_skipped == 2\n\n    def test_integration_subprocess_all_pass_aggregation(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"All 5 tasks pass → PASS status and CONTINUE recommendation.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(5)\n\n        def pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=100, minimum_allocation=5)\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=pass_factory,\n        )\n\n        assert all(r.status == TaskStatus.PASS for r in results)\n        assert remaining == []\n\n        report = aggregate_task_results(1, results, remaining)\n        assert report.status == \"PASS\"\n        md = report.to_markdown()\n        assert \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in md\n        assert \"tasks_total: 5\" in md\n        assert \"tasks_passed: 5\" in md\n\n        # Budget: 5 tasks * 3 turns each = 15 net consumed\n        # available = initial - net consumed = 100 - 15 = 85\n        assert ledger.available() == 85\n\n\nclass TestBackwardCompat:\n    \"\"\"Backward compatibility tests: grace_period=0 produces v1.2.1 behavior.\n\n    Verifies that the default configuration (grace_period=0, GateMode.BLOCKING)\n    produces results identical to pre-trailing-gate behavior with zero\n    additional daemon threads from the gate system. (D-0013)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_backward_compat_grace_period_zero_is_default(self):\n        \"\"\"SprintConfig inherits grace_period=0 from PipelineConfig by default.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import PipelineConfig\n        pc = PipelineConfig()\n        assert pc.grace_period == 0\n\n        config = SprintConfig(\n            index_path=Path(\"/tmp/index.md\"),\n            release_dir=Path(\"/tmp/release\"),\n            phases=[Phase(number=1, file=Path(\"/tmp/p1.md\"))],\n        )\n        assert config.grace_period == 0\n\n    def test_backward_compat_gate_mode_blocking_is_default(self):\n        \"\"\"GateMode.BLOCKING is the default for pipeline steps, matching v1.2.1 behavior.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateMode, Step\n        step = Step(\n            id=\"test\",\n            prompt=\"test\",\n            output_file=Path(\"/tmp/out.txt\"),\n            gate=None,\n            timeout_seconds=60,\n        )\n        assert step.gate_mode == GateMode.BLOCKING\n\n    def test_backward_compat_sprint_pass_grace_period_zero(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Full sprint with grace_period=0 produces identical PASS result to v1.2.1.\"\"\"\n        import threading\n\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        assert config.grace_period == 0  # confirm default\n\n        class _PassPopen:\n            def __init__(self):\n                self.returncode = 0\n                self.pid = 2001\n                self._poll = 0\n\n            def poll(self):\n                self._poll += 1\n                return None if self._poll <= 1 else 0\n\n            def wait(self, timeout=None):\n                self.returncode = 0\n                return 0\n\n        def _factory(*args, **kwargs):\n            phase = config.phases[0]\n            config.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            config.result_file(phase).write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\")\n            config.output_file(phase).write_text(\"output\\n\")\n            return _PassPopen()\n\n        baseline_threads = threading.active_count()\n\n        captured = []\n        with (\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.executor.shutil.which\", return_value=\"/usr/bin/claude\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.pipeline.process.subprocess.Popen\", side_effect=_factory),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.pipeline.process.os.setpgrp\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.notify._notify\"),\n            patch(\"superclaude.cli.sprint.executor.SprintLogger\") as logger_cls,\n        ):\n            logger = MagicMock()\n            logger.write_summary = MagicMock(side_effect=lambda sr: captured.append(sr))\n            logger_cls.return_value = logger\n            execute_sprint(config)\n\n        # After sprint completes, thread count should return to baseline\n        # (the OutputMonitor daemon thread is stopped in the finally block)\n        post_threads = threading.active_count()\n        assert post_threads <= baseline_threads + 1  # allow +1 for monitor cleanup race\n\n        # Sprint result matches v1.2.1 behavior exactly\n        sr = captured[0]\n        assert sr.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS\n        assert len(sr.phase_results) == 1\n        assert sr.phase_results[0].status == PhaseStatus.PASS\n        assert sr.phase_results[0].exit_code == 0\n\n    def test_backward_compat_per_task_no_ledger(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Without a TurnLedger, per-task loop behaves identically to v1.2.1.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = [\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T03.01\", title=\"Task 1\"),\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T03.02\", title=\"Task 2\"),\n            TaskEntry(task_id=\"T03.03\", title=\"Task 3\"),\n        ]\n\n        def pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n        #
